_comment: Lookup table from AST node kind/type to useful information. Keys are 'kind' alone when the kind is sufficient to identify the token, or 'kind/type' when the type sub-discriminator is also needed. Use the pattern (node.type ? `${node.kind}/${node.type}` : node.kind) for O(1) lookup.

compilation_unit.name: file
compilation_unit.description: The root of an Anvil source file. A compilation unit contains all top-level declarations in a `.anvil` file: processes, channel classes, type definitions, functions, constants, and imports.

import_directive.name: import
import_directive.description: An import directive. This pulls all top-level definitions from another Anvil file (or a SystemVerilog file when prefixed with `extern`) into the current compilation unit.

proc_def.name: process
proc_def.description:
A process definition, declaring a hardware module.

Processes communicate through channel endpoints and contain registers, spawned sub-processes, and concurrent threads.

func_def.name: function
func_def.description:
A function definition, declaring an expression-level macro.

Calling a function inlines the body at the call site - functions do not produce hardware modules.

macro_def.name: constant
macro_def.description:
A constant (macro) definition, binding an integer identifier to a compile-time integer value.

Constants can be used wherever an integer literal is expected, such as array sizes.

type_def.name: type
type_def.description: A type alias definition, binding a name to a data type, optionally parameterised.

type_element_def.name: type element
type_element_def.description: A definition of a struct or enum field.

channel_class_def.name: channel class
channel_class_def.description:
A channel class definition, describing a communication contract between two process endpoints.

It declares a set of named messages, each with a direction (`left`/`right`), payload type, lifetime, and synchronisation mode.

message_def.name: message
message_def.description:
A message definition inside a channel class, specifying one communication event. It declares:
 - the direction (`left`/`right`)
 - the payload data type
 - the message lifetime, and 
 - optional synchronisation modes for each side.

The direction marks the side a message is sent to. Thus, a `left` message is sent by senders on the `right` to recipients on the `left`, and vice versa.

A message must be *sustained* for the duration of its declared lifetime for successful communication.

channel_def.name: channel
channel_def.description:
A channel instantiation inside a process body.

This creates a channel instance and binds names to its two endpoints, the implied `left` and `right` sides respectively.
channel_def.examples:
```anvil
chan le -- ri : BusClass;
chan le -- ri : BusClass[4];  // array of 4 channels
```

endpoint_def.name: endpoint
endpoint_def.description:
An endpoint definition, naming one side of a channel connection for a process.

It appears in a process argument list or as the result of a `chan` creation inside a process body, and carries the channel class, direction (`left`/`right`), and optional `foreign` tag.

The direction marks the side this process plays for this channel, so a `left` endpoint definition implies this process is receives incoming `left` messages, and sends outgoing `right` messages (and vice versa).

reg_def.name: register
reg_def.description:
A register declaration, creating a persistent hardware state inside a process.

Registers are initialized to zero on reset. They are read with `*r` and written with `set r := expr`.

sig_def.name: signal
sig_def.description: A signal definition, describing a named value with a specific data type and lifetime contract.

spawn_def.name: spawn
spawn_def.description: A spawn statement, instantiating a child process inside a parent process body, passing channel endpoints as arguments.

shared_var_def.name: shared variable
shared_var_def.description: A shared variable declaration, creating a value that is written by one designated thread and read by others. The `assigned by` clause names the thread index responsible for writing it.

param.name: parameter
param.description: A compile-time parameter on a process, channel class, or type. Parameters come in two kinds: `n : int` (integer constant) and `T : type` (type). They are resolved at compile time and can be used as array sizes or type arguments.

param_value.name: parameter value
param_value.description: A concrete value supplied for a compile-time parameter at the point of instantiation. Parameter values are either integer literals or data types.

param_value/int.name: int parameter value
param_value/int.description: An integer compile-time parameter value, supplied as a numeric literal at the instantiation site (e.g., `spawn P<4>(ep)`).

param_value/type.name: type parameter value
param_value/type.description: A data-type compile-time parameter value, supplied as a type expression at the instantiation site (e.g., `spawn P<logic[8]>(ep)`).

typed_arg.name: argument
typed_arg.description: A named argument of a function definition, optionally carrying a type annotation.

message_specifier.name: message specifier
message_specifier.description: A message specifier refers to a specific message on a specific endpoint, written as `endpoint.messageName` or `endpoint[i].messageName` for indexed endpoints.

data_type.name: type
data_type.description: A data type node describes the shape of a value in the Anvil type system. The `type` field further discriminates the specific form (logic, array, tuple, record, variant, etc.).

data_type/logic.name: logic
data_type/logic.description: The primitive logic type. `logic` represents a single bit; `logic[N]` represents an N-bit logic array. This is the fundamental hardware value type in Anvil.

data_type/array.name: array type
data_type/array.description: An array data type holding `N` elements of a base type. Multidimensional arrays are expressed as `dtype[N][M]`.
data_type/array.examples:
```anvil
reg buf : logic[8][4];  // array of 4 bytes
```

data_type/tuple.name: tuple type
data_type/tuple.description: A tuple type grouping a fixed number of heterogeneous values. The empty tuple `()` is the unit type (also spelled `void` in message signatures).

data_type/record.name: struct
data_type/record.description: A struct (record) data type with named fields. Structs are defined with the `struct` keyword and constructed with `Name::{ field = expr; ... }` syntax.

data_type/variant.name: enum
data_type/variant.description: An enum data type whose value is exactly one of a set of named constructors. Enums are defined with the `enum` keyword and constructed with `Name::VariantName` syntax.

data_type/opaque.name: type parameter
data_type/opaque.description: An opaque (unresolved) type. This references a name that is a type parameter rather than a concrete type.

data_type/named.name: named type
data_type/named.description: A reference to a named type (alias, struct, or enum), optionally supplied with compile-time parameters.

literal.name: literal
literal.description: A numeric literal. It may be binary, decimal, hexadecimal, or an unsized integer.

literal/binary.name: binary literal
literal/binary.description: A sized binary numeric literal of the form `N'b<digits>`, representing the bit-width `N` and binary (base-2) digits of the value.

literal/decimal.name: decimal literal
literal/decimal.description: A sized decimal numeric literal of the form `N'd<digits>`, representing the bit-width `N` and decimal (base-10) digits of the value.

literal/hex.name: hex literal
literal/hex.description: A sized hexadecimal numeric literal of the form `N'h<digits>`, representing the bit-width `N` and hexadecimal (base-16) digits of the value.

literal/with_length.name: sized literal
literal/with_length.description: A numeric literal with an explicit bit width. Its bit-width and value are resolved to a plain integer.

literal/no_length.name: unsized literal
literal/no_length.description: A numeric literal with no explicit bit width. The width is inferred from context. Used in contexts such as array sizes and constant expressions.
literal/no_length.examples:
```anvil
const N = 8;
reg r : logic[8];  // the '8' here
```

expr/literal.name: literal
expr/literal.description: A literal value expression. They are fixed values explicitly denoted in source, such as `8'd42`, `4'b1010`, or `16'hFF00`.

expr/identifier.name: identifier
expr/identifier.description: A reference to a name bound by a `let` binding, process argument, or process-level declaration (register, endpoint, etc.).

expr/Call.name: function call
expr/Call.description: A function call expression. It inlines the body of a `func` definition at the call site.

expr/assign.name: register write
expr/assign.description:
A register write expression. Writes a value to a register or one of its fields/indices.

The write *consumes* a clock cycle, and only takes effect in the **next** clock cycle.

The expression evaluates to `void` *after the delay*.
expr/assign.examples:
```anvil
set r := expr
set r.field := expr
set r[i] := expr
```

expr/binop.name: binary operator
expr/binop.examples:
```anvil
a + b
a == b
a && b
a in {x, y, z}
```

expr/tuple.name: tuple
expr/tuple.description: A tuple construction expression, grouping multiple values. The empty tuple `()` is the unit value.

expr/let.name: let binding
expr/let.description:
A `let` binding, which introduces a name for an expression's result. The continuation of this expression may use the name.

`let x = e1` starts `e1` concurrently with the continuation; `let x = e1 >> e2` waits for `e1` before `e2`.

expr/join.name: join
expr/join.description:
The join (parallel) operator `;`. Starts expressions on both sides simultaneously.

Evaluates to the result of the right-hand side when both complete.

expr/wait.name: wait
expr/wait.description:
The wait (sequencing) operator `>>`. Waits for the left-hand expression to complete, then starts the right-hand expression.

If the left-hand expression *consumes* cycles (note: this is distinct from cycles *sustained* for the lifetime of a message), the right-hand expression will be delayed until the left-hand expression finishes consumption.

Evaluates to the result of the right side.

expr/cycle.name: cycle delay
expr/cycle.description:
A `cycle N` expression. *Consumes* and delays subsequent execution by `N` clock cycles.

Evaluates to `void` *after the delay*.

expr/sync.name: sync
expr/sync.description: A `sync` expression. Synchronises on a shared variable, waiting until the variable has been assigned by its designated thread.

expr/if_expr.name: if expression
expr/if_expr.description:
A conditional expression. The condition must be fully evaluated before the chosen branch begins.

A falsy condition is all-zero bits; truthy is any other value.

The else branch defaults to evaluating to `void` if omitted.

expr/try_recv.name: try recv
expr/try_recv.description: A message receive attempt. If the message is immediately available per timing contracts, binds the received value to a name and evaluates the success branch; otherwise evaluates the failure branch.

expr/try_send.name: try send
expr/try_send.description: A message send attempt. If the receiver is immediately ready per timing contracts, sends the value and evaluates the success branch; otherwise evaluates the failure branch.

expr/construct.name: enum constructor
expr/construct.description: An enum variant construction expression. Creates a value of an enum type by naming the enum and the variant.

expr/record.name: struct literal
expr/record.description: A struct (record) construction expression. Creates a value of a struct type by providing all field values, or updates an existing value with the `with` syntax.

expr/index.name: index
expr/index.description: An indexing expression. Accesses a single element (`e[i]`) or a contiguous range (`e[lo+:sz]`) of an array or logic value.

expr/indirect.name: field access
expr/indirect.description: A field access expression (`.`). Reads a named field of a struct value.

expr/concat.name: concatenation
expr/concat.description: A bit-concatenation expression. Packs multiple values together; the first operand occupies the high bits. The `flat` variant (`#flat{...}`) flattens nested arrays.
expr/concat.examples:
```anvil
#{ high, low }       // high bits first
#flat{ a, b, c }
```

expr/cast.name: cast
expr/cast.description: A type cast expression. Reinterprets an expression as a different data type.

expr/ready.name: ready
expr/ready.description: A `ready` check for messages. Tests whether a message on an endpoint is available to receive, returning a logic value without blocking.

expr/probe.name: probe
expr/probe.description: A `probe` expression. Inspects the state of a message on an endpoint.

expr/match.name: match expression
expr/match.description: A pattern-matching expression that dispatches on an enum value. Each arm binds a pattern; the `_` wildcard arm is mandatory.

expr/read.name: register read
expr/read.description: A register read expression. Reads the current value of a register. The value is valid until the next write to that register.

expr/debug.name: debug operation
expr/debug.description: A debug operation (`dprint` or `dfinish`). Simulation-only - this has no effect on synthesised hardware.

expr/send.name: send
expr/send.description:
A send expression. Sends the given value to the recipient, following the timing contracts for this message.

Attempting a send outside of such agreements results in compile-time errors.

expr/recv.name: recv
expr/recv.description:
A receive expression. Receives the value from the sender, following the timing contracts for this message.

Attempting to receive outside of such agreements results in compile-time errors.

expr/shared_assign.name: shared assign
expr/shared_assign.description: A shared variable assignment (`put`). Makes a new value available for a shared variable, signalling to other threads that synchronise on it.

expr/list.name: array literal
expr/list.description: An array literal expression. Constructs a homogeneous sequence of values.

expr/recurse.name: recurse
expr/recurse.description: A `recurse` expression. This restarts the enclosing `recursive` thread from the beginning. It can appear at any point in the thread body, enabling flexible pipelining and loop control.
expr/recurse.examples:
```anvil
recursive {
    let x = recv ep.req >>
    send ep.resp (x) >>
    recurse
}
```

lvalue.name: lvalue
lvalue.description: An lvalue. A writable target for register write (`set`) or field/index update. Lvalues name registers and allow chained field and index access.

lvalue/reg.name: register lvalue
lvalue/reg.description: A simple register name as a write target.

lvalue/indexed.name: indexed lvalue
lvalue/indexed.description: An indexed element of an array register as a write target.

lvalue/indirected.name: field lvalue
lvalue/indirected.description: A named field of a struct register as a write target.

index.name: index
index.description: An index expression for array access or lvalue indexing.

index/range.name: range index
index/range.description: A range index `e[lo+:sz]` selecting `sz` elements starting at position `lo`.

debug_op.name: debug op
debug_op.description: A debug operation node. Debug operations are simulation-only and do not affect synthesised hardware.

debug_op/debug_print.name: dprint
debug_op/debug_print.description: A `dprint` operation. Prints a formatted string during simulation (does not affect synthesised hardware). Supports printf-style format specifiers.

debug_op/debug_finish.name: dfinish
debug_op/debug_finish.description: A `dfinish` operation. Terminates the simulation (does not affect synthesised hardware).

thread.name: thread
thread.description: A concurrent execution thread inside a process. Process threads are either `loop` (restarts automatically) or `recursive` (restart is explicit via `recurse`). All threads in a process run concurrently.

send_pack.name: send
send_pack.description: A send packet pairing a message specifier with the expression to be transmitted.

recv_pack.name: recv
recv_pack.description: A receive packet naming the message specifier to receive from.

constructor_spec.name: enum constructor
constructor_spec.description: An enum constructor specification naming the enum type and the variant being constructed.

sig_type.name: signal type
sig_type.description: A signal type pairing a data type with a lifetime annotation. Used in channel message definitions to describe what kind of value is transmitted and for how long it remains valid.

sig_lifetime.name: lifetime
sig_lifetime.description: A lifetime annotation on a signal, describing the time window during which the communicated value is guaranteed to be valid.

delay_pat.name: lifetime pattern
delay_pat.description: A delay pattern specifying the ending point of a value's lifetime. Can be a fixed number of cycles, relative to another message, or eternal.

delay_pat/cycles.name: cycle lifetime
delay_pat/cycles.description: A lifetime of exactly `N` cycles. The value must be *sustained* for this duration to keep the communication valid.

delay_pat/message.name: message-relative lifetime
delay_pat/message.description: A lifetime tied to the lifetime of another message, optionally offset by `N` cycles. Such a message must be *sustained* till the other message is exchanged, plus `N` cycles

delay_pat/eternal.name: eternal lifetime
delay_pat/eternal.description: A lifetime that never expires - the value is always valid.

args_spawn.name: spawn argument
args_spawn.description: An argument passed to a `spawn` statement, referring to an endpoint by name or by indexed range.

args_spawn/single.name: endpoint argument
args_spawn/single.description: A single endpoint passed by name to a spawned process.

args_spawn/indexed.name: indexed endpoint argument
args_spawn/indexed.description: An endpoint range passed to a spawned process by index or range.

array_dimensions.name: array dimensions
array_dimensions.description: The dimension specification of an array type or channel array. Can be one-dimensional (`dtype[N]`) or multi-dimensional (`dtype[N][M]`).

singleton_or_list.name: value or list
singleton_or_list.description: An internal AST node that holds either a single value or a list of values. Used in binary operator expressions where the right-hand side may be a set of values (e.g. `in {a, b, c}`).

singleton_or_list/single.name: single value
singleton_or_list/single.description: A single-value variant of a `singleton_or_list` node.

singleton_or_list/list.name: list
singleton_or_list/list.description: A multi-value variant of a `singleton_or_list` node, e.g. the set in `e in {a, b, c}`.

proc_def_body.name: process body
proc_def_body.description: The body of a process definition, containing its channels, registers, spawned sub-processes, shared variables, and concurrent threads.

proc_def_body/native.name: native process body
proc_def_body/native.description: The body of a native Anvil process, written in Anvil source.

proc_def_body/extern.name: external process body
proc_def_body/extern.description: The body of an `extern` process wrapping a SystemVerilog module. Maps Anvil channel messages to SystemVerilog port signals.

cycle_proc.name: cycle process
cycle_proc.description: An internal compiled representation of a process as a clocked state machine - a transition function paired with its signal definitions.

message_sync_mode.name: sync mode
message_sync_mode.description: The synchronisation mode of a channel message, controlling when sends and receives may occur.

message_sync_mode/dynamic.name: dynamic sync
message_sync_mode/dynamic.description: **Dynamic synchronisation** (`@dyn`): handshake-based, using valid/acknowledge signals. The sender and receiver may interact at any time.

message_sync_mode/static.name: static sync
message_sync_mode/static.description: **Static synchronisation** (`@#I~N`): the message is available every `N` cycles, with an optional fixed initial offset of `I` cycles (if omitted, defaults to an offset of `N`).
message_sync_mode/static.examples:
```anvil
@#2     // every 2nd cycle
@#0~4   // every 4 cycles, starting at cycle 0
```

message_sync_mode/dependent.name: dependent sync
message_sync_mode/dependent.description: **Dependent synchronisation** (`@#msg+N`): the message is available relative to another named message by an offset of `N` cycles (if omitted, defaults to no offset).
